profile.title: Workout & Nutrition Guide
profile.subtitle: Personalized for a 55-Year-Old Male
profile.tagline: Back-Safe Training  |  Mediterranean Nutrition  |  16:8 Intermittent Fasting

lowerBackRules: No barbell back squats or conventional deadlifts
All dumbbell work seated or supported — never standing under load with free spine
5-minute core activation warm-up before every session: dead bug + bird-dog + glute bridge
Brace core on every rep of every exercise — treat your trunk like a lifting belt
STOP IMMEDIATELY if lower back recruits — no pushing through it. This rule saves years

workoutDays.title: Chest + Core
workoutDays.note: Core activation warm-up (5 min) before lifting. See Section 4 for protocol.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.cue: Controlled range, back flat to bench, no shoulder impingement

exercises.name: Cable Chest Fly (Low Pulley)
exercises.cue: Arms slightly bent, squeeze chest at center, constant tension

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
exercises.cue: Seat height so handles at mid-chest, back fully supported

exercises.name: Push-Ups
exercises.cue: Straight body line, don't let hips sag

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.cue: Lower back FLAT on floor the entire time — non-negotiable

exercises.name: Forearm Plank
exercises.cue: Neutral spine, glutes squeezed, breathe steadily

workoutDays.title: Legs: Quad & Glute Focus
workoutDays.note: Core activation warm-up (5 min) before lifting. Focus on controlled range — these muscles are being rebuilt from scratch.

exercises.name: Leg Press (Machine)
exercises.cue: Feet mid-plate shoulder-width, never let lower back peel off pad

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.cue: DB at chest, sit back and down, torso upright, knees track over toes

exercises.name: Seated Leg Extension
exercises.cue: Full range, pause at top, slow lower

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.cue: Hips stay on pad, slow eccentric

exercises.name: Hip Thrust (Bench)
exercises.cue: Upper back on bench edge, drive through heels, squeeze glutes 1 sec at top

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.cue: Heel below platform, full extension at top

workoutDays.title: Swim
narrative: Mix freestyle and backstroke. Backstroke is particularly valuable here — it decompresses the lumbar spine and opens the thoracic region in the opposite direction from sitting. On high back-sensitivity days, use a pull buoy for the entire session to take legs out of the equation and keep all rotation gentle.
This session is cardio, active recovery, and back therapy simultaneously. Don't skip it.

workoutDays.title: Biceps + Shoulders + Core
workoutDays.note: Core activation warm-up (5 min). All curls are SEATED — this eliminates the temptation to swing through the lower back.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Curl
exercises.cue: SEATED eliminates back stress, full range, don't swing

exercises.name: Cable Hammer Curl
exercises.cue: Neutral grip, elbows pinned at sides

exercises.name: Machine Preacher Curl
exercises.cue: Arms fully supported, don't hyperextend at bottom

exercises.name: Seated Lateral Raise (Light)
exercises.cue: Lead with elbows, raise to shoulder height only

exercises.name: Cable Face Pull
exercises.cue: Cable at face height, elbows FLARED HIGH AND WIDE — this is not a row

exercises.name: Bird-Dog
exercises.cue: Opposite arm and leg, spine neutral, hold 2 sec, no rotation

workoutDays.title: Legs: Posterior Chain & Hinge
workoutDays.note: Core activation warm-up (5 min). The Romanian Deadlift is included but keep weights LIGHT — this is a hinge pattern, not a strength test. Technique first, always.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift (Light DBs)
exercises.cue: Hinge at HIPS not waist, DBs close to legs, stop at hamstring stretch

exercises.name: Step-Ups (Box or Bench)
exercises.cue: Drive through heel of elevated foot, don't push off back foot

exercises.name: Reverse Lunge
exercises.cue: Step BACK, front knee tracks over toes, torso upright

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.cue: Full range, pause at full flexion, controlled return

exercises.name: Glute Bridge (Bodyweight)
exercises.cue: Feet flat, drive hips up, squeeze glutes 1 full second at top

workoutDays.title: Swim (Longer Session)
narrative: 45-60 minutes. Kick sets, pull buoy intervals, or technique drills. Use this session to work on breathing rhythm and stroke mechanics if desired. Consider water aerobics or aqua jogging for variety while staying gentle on the spine.

workoutDays.title: Active Recovery
narrative: 20-30 minute walk, light stretching, or yoga. Focus on hip flexors, hamstrings, and thoracic rotation. This is not a rest day in the sedentary sense — it's active recovery. Move, but don't load.

nutrition.fastingNote: The 16-hour fast leverages overnight hormone shifts — growth hormone peaks during the fast, and insulin stays low. Breaking the fast at noon keeps the morning productive and simplifies meal timing around training.

meals.name: Meal 1 — 12:00 PM (Break Fast)
items: 4-5 egg whites + 1-2 whole eggs, scrambled in olive oil
1 avocado, sliced — DAILY NON-NEGOTIABLE. Do not remove this.
Olive oil, lemon juice, fresh herbs

meals.name: Meal 2 — 3:00 PM
items: 6-8 oz grilled chicken, salmon, shrimp, or canned tuna
Large salad: arugula, spinach, olives, roasted peppers, red onion
Dressing: olive oil + red wine vinegar + Dijon mustard
Optional: 1/4 cup chickpeas for extra fiber

meals.name: Meal 3 — 6:30–7:30 PM (Largest Meal)
items: 8 oz protein: salmon, sea bass, chicken thigh, lean ground turkey, or shrimp
Roasted vegetables: broccoli, asparagus, zucchini, eggplant, or cauliflower
Training days only: lentils, white beans, or 1/2 cup quinoa as a carb source
Olive oil as cooking fat throughout

meals.name: Snacks (Between Meals, If Needed)
items: Plain full-fat Greek yogurt + blueberries or strawberries
Protein shake — whey or casein, <5g sugar per serving

rules: Avocado: 1 daily — full stop. Healthy fats + fiber + satiety, no debate.
AVOID: white bread, pasta, regular rice, fruit juice, cream-based sauces, fried food
Water: 80-100 oz minimum daily — track it
Electrolyte packet (zero sugar — LMNT or Liquid IV Zero) daily during the fast
Coffee and green tea are fine during the fast — no cream or sugar

preWorkout.name: Dead Bug
preWorkout.note: Lower back flat on floor — always

postWorkout.name: Piriformis Stretch (Figure-4)
postWorkout.notes: Lying on back, cross ankle over opposite knee

postWorkout.name: Child's Pose
postWorkout.notes: Arms extended, breathe into low back

PROTEINS: Chicken breast (2-3 lbs) — boneless skinless, buy in bulk
Canned tuna in olive oil (4-6 cans)
Greek yogurt, plain full-fat (32 oz tub)
Whey or casein protein powder (<5g sugar/serving)
PRODUCE: Broccoli (2 heads or bag of florets)
HYDRATION / ELECTROLYTES: Electrolyte packets, no sugar (LMNT or Liquid IV Zero)
